mission.title: coredns: Add fallthrough to file plugin, or add wildcards to hosts plugin
mission.description: Add fallthrough to file plugin, or add wildcards to hosts plugin. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current coredns deployment
steps.description:
Verify your coredns version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
helm list -n coredns 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working coredns installation.

steps.title: Review coredns configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns
```
**What would you like to be added**:
Either of these:
1. fallthrough for `file` plugin
2.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add fallthrough to file plugin, or add wildcards to hosts…
steps.description:
My thoughts...

The _hosts_ plugin intends to follow the established format of a hosts file, which does not support wildcard notation. https://github.com/coredns/coredns/issues/4516#issuecomment-797362900

Fallthrough behavior has occasionally been requested for the _file_ plugin, but not adopted.
```yaml
template IN ANY x.net {
        match "\w*\.(x.net\.)$"
        answer "{{ .Name }} 3600 IN A 10.2.0.10" 
        fallthrough
    }
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add fallthrough to file plugin, or add wildcards to hosts…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
My thoughts...

The _hosts_ plugin intends to follow the established format of a hosts file, which does not support wildcard notation. https://github.com/coredns/coredns/issues/4516#issuecomment-797362900

Fallthrough behavior has occasionally been requested for the _file_ plugin, but not adopted.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with coredns installed or the issue environment reproducible.
